faqs.question: What quality setting should I use?
faqs.answer: For most web photos, a medium-high quality setting keeps the image looking good while reducing file size.

faqs.question: Can compression damage an image?
faqs.answer: Aggressive lossy compression can reduce quality. Keep an original copy and use moderate settings when quality matters.

faqs.question: Is local compression slower?
faqs.answer: It depends on your device, but it avoids upload time and keeps files private.

faqs.question: Is this image to PDF converter private?
faqs.answer: Yes. The conversion runs in your browser, so your images are not uploaded to Image Tool Stack servers. This is especially helpful for personal documents, IDs, receipts, and confidential screenshots.

faqs.question: Can I use it for scanned documents?
faqs.answer: Yes. If you have photos or screenshots of document pages, you can convert them into one PDF. For best results, crop the edges, keep the page straight, and use bright lighting before converting.

faqs.question: What is the best image format before converting to PDF?
faqs.answer: JPG is usually best for photos and scanned paper documents because it keeps file size smaller. PNG is better for screenshots, text-heavy images, diagrams, and graphics with sharp edges.

faqs.question: Why is my PDF file large?
faqs.answer: PDF size depends mostly on the image resolution and format. If the PDF is too large, resize or compress the images first, then convert the optimized versions to PDF.

faqs.question: Will each image become a separate PDF page?
faqs.answer: Yes. The converter is designed to place images into a clean PDF document so each selected image can appear as its own page.

faqs.question: What is aspect ratio?
faqs.answer: Aspect ratio is the relationship between width and height. Preserving it prevents stretching.

faqs.question: Can resizing improve speed?
faqs.answer: Yes. Smaller dimensions usually mean fewer bytes and faster image loading.

faqs.answer: The JPG format does not support transparency. To maintain the invisible background, the image must be saved in the PNG or WebP format.

faqs.answer: Yes, our AI is trained on millions of images and can handle complex backgrounds, though solid contrast between the subject and background yields the sharpest edges.

faqs.question: How does an AI upscaler work?
faqs.answer: Unlike traditional resizers that just stretch existing pixels, AI upscalers use neural networks trained on millions of high-res photos to mathematically predict and generate missing details.

faqs.question: Why is my browser getting slow during upscaling?
faqs.answer: Running complex machine learning models requires significant RAM and GPU power. If your device is older, the process may take up to a minute and temporarily slow down your browser.

faqs.question: Can I upscale an already large image?
faqs.answer: It is not recommended. Upscaling is designed for small images (under 1000px). Trying to 4x upscale an image that is already 4K may crash your browser due to memory limits.

faqs.question: Is cropping the same as resizing?
faqs.answer: No. Cropping cuts off parts of the image to change the framing. Resizing scales the entire image down to fit smaller dimensions.

faqs.question: Why does the file size change after cropping?
faqs.answer: Because you are discarding pixels from the original image, the resulting file will contain less data and naturally have a smaller file size in KB.

faqs.question: How do I calculate a percentage increase?
faqs.answer: Subtract the original value from the new value, divide the result by the original value, and multiply by 100. For example, if a price increases from $40 to $50, the change is ($50 - $40) / $40 * 100 = 25%.

faqs.question: Can I use this calculator for discounts?
faqs.answer: Yes. To find a discount, calculate the percentage value (e.g., 20% of $80 = $16) and subtract it from the original price ($80 - $16 = $64).

faqs.question: Does this calculator process data on a server?
faqs.answer: No. Like all tools on our platform, the percentage calculations run entirely inside your browser's memory. No data is sent to external servers.

faqs.question: ¿Cómo calculo un aumento porcentual?
faqs.answer: Reste el valor original del nuevo valor, divida el resultado por el valor original y multiplique por 100. Por ejemplo, si un precio aumenta de $40 a $50, el cambio es ($50 - $40) / $40 * 100 = 25%.

faqs.answer: Calculamos o tempo de leitura usando uma velocidade média de 275 palavras por minuto (wpm) e o tempo de fala usando 150 palavras por minuto.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the days between two dates?
faqs.answer: Our date calculator translates both dates into UTC milliseconds, subtracts the start time from the end time, and converts the resulting difference into days, weeks, hours, and minutes.

faqs.question: Can I add or subtract business days only?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can select parameters to exclude weekends (Saturdays and Sundays) to find the exact business day difference.

faqs.question: What happens if I cross timezone boundaries?
faqs.answer: To prevent timezone shifts from altering your day count, all calculations are normalized to Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) before subtraction.

benefits.title: Seguridad de huso horario

faqs.answer: Nuestra calculadora traduce ambas fechas a milisegundos UTC, resta el tiempo inicial del final y convierte la diferencia en días y semanas.

faqs.question: ¿Puedo sumar o restar días hábiles únicamente?
faqs.answer: Sí. Puede seleccionar parámetros para excluir fines de semana (sábados y domingos) para encontrar la diferencia exacta.

faqs.question: ¿Qué pasa si cruzo límites de huso horario?
faqs.answer: Para evitar alteraciones por huso horario, todos los cálculos se normalizan a UTC antes de la resta.

faqs.answer: Notre calculatrice convertit les dates en millisecondes UTC, soustrait la première de la seconde et convertit le résultat en jours.

faqs.question: Puis-je compter uniquement les jours ouvrables ?
faqs.answer: Oui. Vous pouvez exclure les week-ends (samedis et dimanches) pour obtenir uniquement les jours ouvrés.

faqs.question: Que se passe-t-il avec les fuseaux horaires ?
faqs.answer: Pour éviter les décalages, tous les calculs sont normalisés en temps universel coordonné (UTC).

faqs.question: Wie berechne ich die Tage zwischen zwei Daten?
faqs.answer: Unser Datumsrechner wandelt beide Daten in UTC-Millisekunden um, subtrahiert sie voneinander und rechnet die Differenz in Tage um.

faqs.answer: Ja. Sie können Wochenenden ausschließen, um die genaue Anzahl der Werktage zu ermitteln.

faqs.question: What is a cryptographic hash generator?
faqs.answer: It takes an input string or file and applies a mathematical hashing algorithm to produce a fixed-length hexadecimal signature. This is a one-way process and cannot be reversed.

faqs.question: Are my files uploaded to generate checksums?
faqs.answer: No. The tool reads the file locally into browser memory using the FileReader API and hashes it on your CPU. No files or text are sent to a server.

faqs.question: What is HMAC?
faqs.answer: Hash-based Message Authentication Code (HMAC) is a specific type of message authentication code involving a cryptographic hash function and a secret cryptographic key, used to verify both data integrity and authenticity.

faqs.question: ¿Qué es un generador de hash criptográfico?
faqs.answer: Toma una cadena de entrada o un archivo y aplica un algoritmo matemático para producir una firma de longitud fija. Es un proceso de una sola vía.

faqs.question: ¿Se cargan mis archivos para generar sumas de comprobación?
faqs.answer: No. La herramienta lee el archivo localmente en la memoria del navegador y realiza el hash en su CPU.

faqs.question: ¿Qué es HMAC?
faqs.answer: El Código de autenticación de mensajes basado en hash es un tipo específico de código que involucra una función hash criptográfica y una clave secreta.

faqs.question: Apa itu metadata EXIF?
faqs.answer: EXIF adalah metadata yang tertanam di dalam gambar oleh kamera dan ponsel pintar, menyimpan lokasi GPS, stempel waktu, dan deskripsi perangkat.

faqs.question: Apakah gambar saya diunggah untuk membaca atau menghapus metadata?
faqs.answer: Tidak. Skrip parsing dan penghapusan berjalan sepenuhnya di dalam browser web Anda.

faqs.question: Format gambar apa saja yang didukung?
faqs.answer: Alat ini mendukung format JPEG, JPG, PNG, dan WebP.

benefits.title: Resize batches for real workflows
benefits.description: Prepare dozens of product photos, blog images, thumbnails, or document uploads with one consistent set of dimensions.

benefits.title: No server upload
benefits.description: The resize work happens inside your browser, which is safer for client files, private photos, IDs, and unreleased product images.

benefits.title: Cleaner site performance
benefits.description: Resizing oversized images before publishing them can reduce page weight and help pages load faster.

howTo.title: Set dimensions
howTo.description: Enter the target size or choose a resize mode that keeps your images proportional.

howTo.title: Export resized files
howTo.description: Resize locally and download the finished image set.

faqs.question: Is batch resizing safe for private images?
faqs.answer: Yes. Files are handled locally in your browser, so they do not need to be uploaded to a remote server.

faqs.question: What should I resize before uploading to a website?
faqs.answer: Resize very large camera photos, product photos, screenshots, and hero images to the maximum display size your site actually needs.

faqs.question: Can bulk resizing help with AdSense and SEO?
faqs.answer: Indirectly, yes. Smaller properly sized images improve user experience and can support better Core Web Vitals.

benefits.title: Batch workflow, one setting
benefits.description: Apply the same dimensions to many files instead of repeating the same resize task image by image.

benefits.title: Useful for product and content teams
benefits.description: Standardize images for ecommerce, blogs, portfolios, directories, and form submissions.

benefits.title: Local and private
benefits.description: Images are processed in the browser, so sensitive batches stay on your device.

howTo.title: Choose images
howTo.description: Select the files you want to resize together.

howTo.title: Pick the output size
howTo.description: Use exact dimensions or preserve aspect ratio depending on the destination.

howTo.title: Download results
howTo.description: Export the resized files and use them wherever needed.

faqs.question: How many images should I resize at once?
faqs.answer: Use batches your device can comfortably handle. Very large files or hundreds of images may take more memory.

faqs.question: Will images be stretched?
faqs.answer: Use aspect ratio preserving options to avoid distortion.

faqs.question: Should I compress after resizing?
faqs.answer: If upload size still matters, resize first and compress the resized output afterward.

faqs.question: Why do iPhones use HEIC?
faqs.answer: HEIC stores high quality photos at smaller sizes, but not every service supports it.

faqs.question: Will the JPG work on Windows?
faqs.answer: Yes. JPG is widely supported on Windows, Android, macOS, browsers, and printers.

faqs.question: Can I convert private photos?
faqs.answer: Yes, because the conversion is handled locally in the browser.

faqs.question: Will PNG make my file smaller?
faqs.answer: Not usually for photos. PNG is often larger than JPG but can preserve sharp details better.

faqs.question: Can PNG have transparency?
faqs.answer: PNG supports transparency, but converting a normal JPG does not automatically remove the background.

faqs.question: When should I use PNG?
faqs.answer: Use PNG for screenshots, graphics, text-heavy images, icons, and transparent assets.

faqs.question: Is PDF to JPG private?
faqs.answer: Yes. The PDF is handled in your browser, so it does not need to be uploaded.

faqs.question: Is JPG good for text pages?
faqs.answer: JPG works, but PNG can be sharper for text-heavy pages and diagrams.

faqs.question: Can I use JPG pages in social posts?
faqs.answer: Yes. Converting a PDF page to JPG makes it easier to share as an image.

faqs.question: Why choose PNG over JPG for PDF pages?
faqs.answer: PNG preserves text and line details better, while JPG is usually smaller for photos.

faqs.question: Will my PDF be uploaded?
faqs.answer: No. The conversion happens inside your browser.

faqs.question: Can PNG files be large?
faqs.answer: Yes. PNG files may be larger, especially for high-resolution pages.

faqs.question: Does JPG support transparency?
faqs.answer: No. Transparent PNG areas become a solid background after conversion.

faqs.question: Why is JPG smaller than PNG?
faqs.answer: JPG uses lossy compression that is efficient for photos and complex colors.

faqs.question: Should screenshots be JPG?
faqs.answer: Usually no. PNG is often better for screenshots and text-heavy graphics.

faqs.question: Can photos contain GPS data?
faqs.answer: Yes. Some photos contain location coordinates, depending on camera and phone settings.

faqs.question: Does removing EXIF reduce quality?
faqs.answer: The visible image should stay the same; the hidden metadata is removed.

faqs.question: Should I check metadata before sharing?
faqs.answer: Yes, especially for photos from phones or cameras with location services enabled.

faqs.answer: No. Metadata is read in your browser.

faqs.question: Can metadata reveal location?
faqs.answer: Sometimes. Photos may contain GPS coordinates if location tagging was enabled.

faqs.question: Can I remove metadata after viewing it?
